title: Phenoxy Acetyl Acetophenone as an Analytical Reagent for Iron
author: D. P. AMBHORE
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Chemistry Department, Nagpur University, Nagpur 440 010 

  Manuscript received 16 August 1981, revised 2 February 1982  

 A new spectrophotometric method for the microdetermination of iron using phenoxy acetyl acetophenone is described. The complex is formed instantaneously, in cold and at pH 5.5. It can be extracted in toluene and shows maximum absorbance at 390 nm. Beer's law is valid over the range of 0.5-8.0 µg Fe/ml. The complex is stable and sensitivity of determination is 0.0081 µg Fe/cm 2 . Many common ions are tolerated to a  large extent.  The method is simple and can be   used satisfactorily for the determination of iron in drugs and ores.
